source.description: Generated raster lesson rebuilt after review so the snail, shell, and leaf read clearly.
source.notes: The v1 finished image remains in assets for history, but the public page and plan use the v2 master.

process_strategy: Keep the shell circle, low body, head, eyestalks, and leaf aligned across every frame, then finish with tan shell and green leaf shading.

final_elements.name: tilted shell circle
final_elements.description: The large tilted circle holding the shell.

final_elements.name: low body guide
final_elements.description: The long low guide under the shell.

final_elements.name: small head oval
final_elements.description: The small head shape in front.

final_elements.name: leaf guide
final_elements.description: The pointed leaf placement beneath the snail.

final_elements.name: shell spiral
final_elements.description: The spiral rings inside the shell.

final_elements.name: soft crawling body
final_elements.description: The rounded head, back, and flat foot.

final_elements.name: eyestalks and eyes
final_elements.description: The two raised eyestalks with small eye ovals.

final_elements.name: leaf veins
final_elements.description: The interior vein marks on the leaf.

frames.visible_job: Places the tilted shell circle, low belly curve, and small head oval before adding the spiral.

frames.step_name: Turn the circle into a shell
frames.visible_job: Darkens the shell edge and adds the central spiral rings inside the same tilted circle.
Builds the low body, rounded head, and flat crawling foot under the finished shell placement.
Adds the two eyestalks, tiny eye ovals, and the pointed leaf under the same crawling foot.

final_step.visible_job: Adds tan shell strokes, soft body shading, green leaf color, and final keeper contours without moving the snail.

rejection_checks: The shell spiral must stay inside the tilted circle established in step two.
The leaf must sit under the same foot line introduced before the final step.
The eyestalks must remain visible and curious in the finished image.
Do not use the archived v1 final as the public finished asset.
